list.title: Germplasm Management
list.titleHelp: Select species first, system defaults to the latest import batch, then search by keyword
list.noBatchAvailable: No import batches available for this species
list.placeholderKeyword: Enter accession, name or parent ID

detail.title: Germplasm Detail
detail.noDynamicFields: No dynamic fields in this batch
detail.noFieldSchema: No dynamic field schema recorded for this batch
detail.noParam: No valid germplasm detail parameters provided, please access from the germplasm list
detail.notReferenced: This germplasm has not been referenced by breeding materials yet
detail.detailPageDescription: View germplasm basic info, lineage neighbors, and breeding program back-references.

import.title: Import Germplasm
import.downloadHint: Download the standard template and a test dataset organized for rice to verify the import flow locally.
import.templateNote2: Rows starting with # are treated as comment rows and may remain in the file.
import.templateNote3: The official header must include ID and chinese_name columns.
import.templateNote4: The taxonomy anchor is selected on this page, so Excel does not need an extra species column.
import.templateNote5: Columns after M_name are not predefined and are all treated as custom fields for this batch.
import.templateNote6: Custom columns in the example template are samples only and do not represent a fixed schema.
import.testSuggestion: Test tip: To quickly verify the page flow, download the 'Rice Demo Data', then select Oryza sativa as the taxonomy anchor on this page before uploading.
import.taxonomyHint: Searches the local taxonomy master database. Enter scientific name or common name first; Chinese aliases need local alias dictionary supplement.
import.taxonomyPlaceholder: Search scientific name / common name / tax id
import.noTaxonomySelected: Please select a species anchor first
import.fileRequirements: File requirements: Must be Excel with headers matching the current template. Validation checks fixed fields, duplicate headers, empty headers with data, duplicate accessions, self-referencing parents, and duplicate accessions within the species.
import.validationFailed: Validation failed, please fix the file and re-upload.
import.fillConventionDesc: The first few rows of the example file use #title, #description, #required, #custom_fields, #notes comment rows to carry documentation. The system skips these rows during import and only recognizes official headers and data rows.
import.importScopeDesc: One Excel file imports one species only. The species is determined by the taxonomy anchor selected on the page, so no species column is needed in the sheet. Do not mix multiple species in one workbook.
import.dynamicFieldRuleDesc: Fixed fields end at M_name. All columns after M_name are imported as batch dynamic fields without platform pre-registration.
import.templateHelperText: Download the example template first, then organize your germplasm Excel in the same structure.
import.selectSpeciesAndUpload: Please select a species and upload a file first
import.validationSuccess: Validation passed, you can proceed with import
import.validationNotPassed: Validation failed, please fix the file and re-upload
import.noTemplateExample: No example file available for the current template
import.defaultFieldNote: Rows starting with # are automatically ignored
import.descriptionHero: Currently supporting the crop_germplasm_v1 template. The example template follows the NCBI SRA annotation style, allowing comment and header rows starting with # at the top; the system ignores these comment rows and only validates official headers and data rows. The official header is divided into 'fixed fields' and 'batch custom fields', where columns after M_name are all treated as dynamic fields.
import.tfFieldId: Germplasm accession ID, must be unique within the species. Use a stable accession code.
import.tfFieldChineseName: Germplasm display name, shown by default in list and detail pages.
import.tfFieldEnglishName: English name or internationally recognized name.
import.tfFieldP: Father accession ID for lineage construction.
import.tfFieldM: Mother accession ID for lineage construction.
import.tfFieldPName: Father name snapshot for manual verification during import.
import.tfFieldMName: Mother name snapshot for manual verification during import.
import.cfRuleStartPosition: Starting from the column after M_name, all are treated as batch custom fields.
import.cfRuleFieldNaming: Column names are decided by the submitter. No pre-registration or consistency with historical batches is required.
import.cfRuleFieldValue: Stored as text by default and added to the batch dynamic attribute area after import.
import.cfRuleEmptyHeader: If a column has data, it must have a header; empty headers with values will cause validation failure.
import.cfExEmptyHeader: Cannot have blank column name with data below
import.vrRuleRequiredFixed: ID and chinese_name columns must exist and cannot be empty in valid data rows.
import.vrRuleDuplicateHeader: Duplicate column names are not allowed in official header rows.
import.vrRuleEmptyHeader: Empty header columns are allowed, but if the column has data values, it will report an error requiring a column name or column removal.
import.vrRuleIdUnique: Accession IDs cannot be duplicated within the same Excel file or within the same taxonomy species.
import.vrRuleSelfRef: P or M cannot equal the current row's own ID; if P and M are identical, a warning is given.
import.vrRuleCommentRow: Rows starting with # are automatically ignored and do not participate in data validation.

batch.title: Import Records
batch.placeholderSearch: Search batch code, file name, template
batch.deleteConfirmContent: This will delete all germplasm records and lineage relationships imported in this batch and mark the batch status as deleted. Batch audit records will be preserved.
batch.description: Review every germplasm import batch, validation results and source files; jump directly to the germplasm list generated by the batch when needed.

graph.title: Germplasm Knowledge Graph
graph.heroDescription: Bring parents, offspring and current batch materials into a single view to help breeders quickly see material origins, lineage extensions and core nodes.
graph.wholeBatchHint: Currently viewing the internal lineage of the whole batch; external nodes are not expanded.
graph.externalParentInLineage: External parents appearing only in lineage
graph.clickNodeHint: Click a node to view parent and offspring details on the right
graph.noRemarkHint: No remark information yet; you can continue adding material descriptions.
graph.noOffspringInView: No offspring materials visible in the current view
graph.clickToViewHint: Click a material in the graph to view lineage details here
graph.missingTaxonomyWarning: Missing taxonomy anchor, cannot load lineage
graph.keepOneScopeWarning: Keep at least one lineage expansion scope
